factors.summary: The company has experienced disruptions due to global health crises, particularly the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: Although most governments have eased or eliminated their restrictions on travel and social interactions, and lifted non-essential business closures, several jurisdictions in which we have operations, such as China, have public health and government mandates that restrict business activities. These mandates and restrictions have, and could continue to have, an impact on our business and operations, and on the operations of some of our suppliers.
evidence.subsection: The effects of global climate change or other unexpected events, including global health crises, may disrupt our operations and have a negative impact on our business.

factors.summary: Restructuring and acquisition expenses have increased, impacting margins.

evidence.text: These decreases were partially offset by pricing and productivity improvements in excess of inflation and both prior year and current year acquisition and divestiture activity.

factors.summary: The company has reduced its share buybacks compared to the prior year, indicating a shift in focus towards other uses of capital.

evidence.text: Cash used to repurchase shares was $351.8 million lower in 2022 compared to 2021.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has decreased, primarily due to changes in working capital and increased investing activities related to acquisitions.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities for the year ended December 31, 2022, decreased by $29.1 million compared to 2021, driven primarily by changes in working capital. Net cash used in investing activities for the year ended December 31, 2022, increased by $962.5 million compared to 2021, primarily due to $923.1 million of cash paid for our acquisition of the Access Technologies business.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin non-residential products and services.

evidence.text: Net revenues from non-residential products [...] increased by a low twenties percent compared to the prior year, driven by improved pricing and higher volumes. [...] Net revenues from residential products decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year.

factors.summary: Non-residential end markets are growing strongly, while residential and some international markets are slowing.

evidence.text: Throughout 2022 we experienced strong demand for our non-residential products and services in our Allegion Americas segment. [...] We also experienced a softening of demand throughout many of the Eurozone economies during the second half of 2022, reflecting increased economic and geopolitical concerns in this region, which impacted several of our businesses in our Allegion International segment.

factors.summary: Management expects continued pricing momentum to drive future revenue growth, but faces risks from macroeconomic and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: We expect this pricing momentum to continue to drive revenue growth and help offset the impact of inflation into 2023. [...] The macroeconomic and geopolitical trends and uncertainties noted above will likely continue to affect us in numerous and evolving ways, the full impact of which on our business, financial condition and results of operations will continue to depend on future developments that are beyond our control and we may not be able to accurately predict.

factors.summary: Inflation is a significant concern, particularly in raw materials and labor costs, which may negatively impact margins if not offset by pricing.

evidence.text: Elevated levels of inflation, including rising prices for raw materials, parts and components, freight, packaging, labor and energy, increases our costs to manufacture and distribute our products and services, and we may be unable to pass these increased costs on to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rate fluctuations are affecting the company's financial results, particularly due to a significant portion of revenue derived from outside the U.S.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rate fluctuations have had, and may continue to have, an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows. Approximately 25% of our 2022 Net revenues were derived outside the U.S., and we expect sales to non-U.S. customers to continue to represent a significant portion of our consolidated Net revenues. Although we may enter into currency exchange contracts to reduce our risk related to currency exchange fluctuations, changes in the relative fair values of currencies occur from time to time and in some instances, as was the case in 2022, have had a significant impact on our Net revenues. We do not hedge against all our currency exposure, and therefore, our results of operations will continue to be susceptible to impacts from currency fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Currency exchange rate fluctuations have had, and may continue to have, an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, such as trade tensions and sanctions, are impacting the company's global operations and supply chain.

evidence.text: Our global operations are subject to multiple risks that are inherent in operating and sourcing globally, including: Changes to trade agreements, sanctions, import and export regulations, including imposition of burdensome tariffs and quotas, and customs duties; Changes in applicable tax regulations and interpretations; Economic downturns; Social and political unrest, instability, national and international conflict, including war, border closures, civil disturbances, terrorist acts and other geographical disputes and uncertainties; Government measures to restrict business activity, for example, to prevent the spread of a communicable disease; Changes in laws and regulations or imposition of currency restrictions and other restraints in various jurisdictions; Limitation of ownership rights, including expropriation of assets by a local government, and limitation on the ability to repatriate earnings; Sovereign debt crises and currency instability in developed and developing countries; Difficulty in staffing and managing global operations; Difficulty in enforcing agreements, collecting receivables and protecting assets through non-U.S. legal systems; and Difficulty in transporting materials, components and products.
evidence.subsection: Our global operations subject us to economic risks.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying due to factors such as increased competition and market dynamics.

evidence.text: Our business operations and performance have been, and are expected to continue to be, impacted by global macroeconomic factors. Ongoing macroeconomic challenges could adversely impact our business, results of operations, financial conditions and cash flows. Macroeconomic challenges, including ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers. These challenges may also make it more challenging for us to manufacture and deliver products to our customers, could cause periodic production interruptions and supply constraints, impact our ability to forecast and plan for future business activities and, if not adequately managed, could have a material adverse impact on our business, results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to factors such as labor shortages, wage inflation, and employee turnover, which are putting pressure on the company's margins.

evidence.text: Labor shortages and increased turnover rates have led to, and could in the future lead to, increased costs, such as increased overtime to meet customer demand and increased wage rates to attract and retain employees.
evidence.subsection: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business depends on our ability to recruit and retain a highly qualified and diverse workforce.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations and compliance are creating significant costs and operational constraints, with the company facing potential liabilities from past and future releases of hazardous substances.

evidence.text: Our U.S. and non-U.S. operations are subject to a number of laws and regulations, including fire and building codes and EHS standards. We have incurred, and will be required to continue to incur, significant expenditures to comply with these laws and regulations.
evidence.subsection: Our operations are subject to regulatory risks.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly related to environmental and ESG practices, are creating new costs and operational constraints, and the company faces risks from potential changes in tax legislation and trade policies.

evidence.text: Changes to, or changes in interpretations of, current laws and regulations, including climate change legislation or other environmental mandates, could require us to increase our compliance expenditures, cause us to significantly alter or discontinue offering existing products and services or cause us to develop new products and services.
evidence.subsection: Our operations are subject to regulatory risks.

factors.summary: The company is investing in improving its IT systems, which includes digital automation and technological applications, to enhance operational efficiency and address risks.

evidence.text: We have invested and will continue to invest in improving our IT Systems. Some of these investments are significant and impact many important operational processes and procedures.
evidence.subsection: We may be subject to risks relating to our information technology and operational technology systems.

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks related to rising interest rates, which could increase its borrowing costs and negatively impact demand for its products. Additionally, the company is exposed to variable interest rate risk, as its outstanding variable rate balance of $306.5 million at the end of 2022 has already led to increased interest expenses due to rising rates.

evidence.text: The 2021 Credit Facilities had a combined outstanding variable rate balance of $306.5 million at December 31, 2022, which exposes us to variable interest rate risk. Applicable variable interest rates have increased throughout 2022, resulting in increased Interest expense.
evidence.subsection: There are risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness.

evidence.text: Macroeconomic challenges, including ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.

factors.summary: The company has recently completed a significant acquisition of the Access Technologies business, which has impacted its cash flows and borrowings. However, the success of future acquisitions is uncertain, and the company may face challenges in achieving the anticipated strategic and financial benefits from these activities.

evidence.text: In July 2022, we completed the acquisition of the Access Technologies business. However, we cannot provide assurance that we will identify or successfully complete acquisitions with suitable candidates in the future, nor can we provide assurance that completed or future acquisitions will be successful or otherwise achieve the anticipated strategic and financial benefits.
evidence.subsection: Changes in customer and consumer preferences and the inability to maintain beneficial relationships with large customers could adversely affect our business.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities for the year ended December 31, 2022, increased by $962.5 million compared to 2021, primarily due to $923.1 million of cash paid for our acquisition of the Access Technologies business.

On July 5, 2022, we completed the acquisition of the Access Technologies business for a closing purchase price of $923.1 million. [...] In September 2022, we sold Milre Systek Co. Ltd. [...] for an immaterial amount.
evidence.subsection: Acquisition of the Access Technologies business

factors.summary: The company has a significant amount of debt, with total borrowings outstanding rising from approximately $1.45 billion to $2.1 billion. This increase is due to new senior notes and borrowings under the senior unsecured revolving credit facility.

evidence.text: We had approximately $2.1 billion of outstanding indebtedness at December 31, 2022. Included in this total was $69 million outstanding under our senior unsecured revolving credit facility (the '2021 Revolving Facility') that permits borrowings of up to $500 million.
evidence.subsection: There are risks associated with our outstanding and future indebtedness.

evidence.text: Total borrowings outstanding 2,106.7 1,450.3 3.200% Senior Notes due 2024 400.0 400.0 3.550% Senior Notes due 2027 400.0 400.0 3.500% Senior Notes due 2029 400.0 400.0 5.411% Senior Notes due 2032 600.0 — Other debt 0.2 0.3 Total borrowings outstanding 2,106.7 1,450.3

factors.summary: The company is facing significant challenges due to unfavorable economic conditions, including inflation, higher interest rates, and geopolitical instability. These factors are creating headwinds across various segments, impacting sales volumes and operational efficiency. Additionally, economic uncertainty and potential downturns pose risks to the company's financial health, potentially leading to impairment charges.

evidence.text: Significant disruptions to our business or end market conditions, protracted economic weakness (including a potential economic downturn or recession), unexpected significant declines in operating results of reporting units, divestitures or market capitalization declines may result in recognition of impairment charges to our goodwill, indefinite-lived intangible or other long-lived assets.
evidence.subsection: We may be required to recognize impairment charges for our goodwill, indefinite-lived intangible assets and other long-lived assets.

evidence.text: A combination of elevated inflation and lower consumer sentiment impacted sales volumes of residential products within our Allegion Americas segment.

The macroeconomic and geopolitical trends and uncertainties noted above will likely continue to affect us in numerous and evolving ways, the full impact of which on our business, financial condition and results of operations will continue to depend on future developments that are beyond our control and we may not be able to accurately predict.

Ongoing macroeconomic challenges could adversely impact our business, results of operations, financial condition and cash flows. Macroeconomic challenges, including ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages, cost inflation, rising interest rates and volatility in the capital markets, have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with deceleration observed in the Eurozone and China, particularly affecting residential products, while non-residential products continue to show strong demand.

evidence.text: Softening demand throughout much of the Eurozone in 2022 has impacted several of our businesses in our Allegion International segment. Additionally, COVID-19 related lockdowns in China throughout the year also contributed to lower volumes.

Throughout 2022 we experienced strong demand for our non-residential products and services in our Allegion Americas segment. [...] While 2022 began with similar strong demand for our residential products in our Allegion Americas segment, macroeconomic conditions had a more challenging impact on demand as the year progressed.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different product categories and regions, with declines in certain areas offset by growth in others.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was driven by lower volumes and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, due to the strengthening of the U.S. dollar relative to most of the currencies in which we do business throughout our Allegion International segment.

Net revenues from non-residential products [...] increased by a low twenties percent compared to the prior year, driven by improved pricing and higher volumes. [...] Net revenues from residential products [...] decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, successfully raising prices to offset lower volumes and unfavorable currency exchange rates. These initiatives contributed to revenue growth and helped mitigate inflationary pressures on costs.

evidence.text: These decreases were partially offset by improved pricing.

Further, in response to the persistent, elevated levels of inflation seen throughout the year, we implemented a series of pricing initiatives across our global businesses. [...] These pricing initiatives significantly contributed to revenue growth in 2022, they also helped mitigate the inflationary pressures on our cost base.

factors.summary: Organic growth is decelerating across multiple segments, driven by factors such as lower volumes, unfavorable currency exchange rates, challenges in the residential market, and economic conditions. Additionally, the company faces risks related to its ability to recruit and retain a skilled workforce, which is crucial for sustaining growth.

evidence.text: The decrease in Net revenues was driven by lower volumes and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, due to the strengthening of the U.S. dollar relative to most of the currencies in which we do business throughout our Allegion International segment.

Net revenues from residential products [...] decreased by a low single digits percent compared to the prior year. [...] During the second half of 2022, we experienced a softening in market demand for our residential products, due in part to elevated inflation and lower consumer sentiment.

Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business is dependent upon our ability to recruit and retain a workforce with the skills necessary to develop, manufacture and deliver the products and services desired by our customers.
evidence.subsection: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business depends on our ability to recruit and retain a highly qualified and diverse workforce.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation and contingent liability risks, which could negatively impact its financial performance and operations. These risks include various ongoing litigations and administrative proceedings related to environmental, asbestos, product liability, intellectual property, and environmental regulations. The resolution of these matters is expected to take a long time and involve uncertainties in estimating future costs.

evidence.text: We are involved in various litigation, claims and administrative proceedings, including those related to environmental, asbestos-related and product liability matters. We believe that these liabilities are subject to the uncertainties inherent in estimating future costs for contingent liabilities and will likely be resolved over an extended period of time.
We are currently, and may in the future become, involved in legal proceedings, claims and disputes incidental to the operation of our business in the ordinary course.
evidence.subsection: Material legal judgments, fines, penalties or settlements imposed against us or our assets could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

factors.summary: The order backlog is not explicitly addressed in the provided subsections, but delays and supply chain challenges suggest potential inefficiencies that could impact the ability to meet customer demand.

evidence.text: Order backlog is not explicitly addressed, but there are delays and shortages affecting supply chain efficiency.

While supply chain challenges around the availability of electronic parts and components persist, and will likely continue to impact our ability to meet the elevated levels of demand for our electronic security products into 2023, we remain focused on providing exceptional service and innovation to our customers.
evidence.subsection: We may be subject to risks relating to our information technology and operational technology systems.

evidence.text: The text does not provide specific information on order backlog or book-to-bill trends.
evidence.subsection: We may be subject to risks relating to our information technology and operational technology systems.

factors.summary: The company is facing rising input costs due to inflation and supply chain disruptions, particularly affecting raw materials and labor. While the company has implemented pricing initiatives to partially offset these costs, there are risks to margins if these measures are insufficient.

evidence.text: Pricing and productivity in excess of inflation 23.7 2.4 % Volume / product mix (15.6) (1.5) % Currency exchange rates (17.6) (1.3) % Investment spending (5.5) (0.7) % Acquisitions / divestitures 2.1 0.3 % Restructuring / acquisition expenses (1.2) (0.1) %

Throughout 2022 we experienced strong demand for our non-residential products and services in our Allegion Americas segment. [...] Further, in response to the persistent, elevated levels of inflation seen throughout the year, we implemented a series of pricing initiatives across our global businesses. [...] These pricing initiatives significantly contributed to revenue growth in 2022, they also helped mitigate the inflationary pressures on our cost base.

Elevated levels of inflation, including rising prices for raw materials, parts and components, freight, packaging, labor and energy, increases our costs to manufacture and distribute our products and services.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

evidence.text: Our ability to meet our customers' needs and achieve cost targets depends on our ability to maintain key manufacturing and supply arrangements.
evidence.subsection: Disruptions in our global supply chain, including product manufacturing and logistical services provided by our supplier partners, may negatively impact our business.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing margin compression across multiple areas, including operating and gross margins. Key factors contributing to this include unfavorable volume/product mix, foreign currency exchange rate movements, increased investment spending, restructuring expenses, higher costs, and supply chain disruptions. Additionally, inflation and supply chain issues are putting further pressure on margins, and the company's ability to maintain or improve margins depends on successful cost management and pricing strategies.

evidence.text: The decreases in Segment operating income and Segment operating margin were primarily driven by unfavorable volume/product mix, unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, increased investment spending and a year-over-year increase in restructuring and acquisition expenses.

Cost of goods sold as a percentage of Net revenues increased to 59.6% from 58.0%, as compared to the year ended December 31, 2021, due to the following: [...] The decrease in Operating margin was primarily due to the year-over-year increase in restructuring and acquisition expenses, unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, increased investment spending and the dilutive impact to Operating margin from our Access Technologies business.

The pricing of some materials, parts and components we use is based on market prices. To mitigate this exposure, we may use annual price contracts to minimize the impact of inflation and to benefit from deflation. Additionally, we are exposed to fluctuations in other costs such as packaging, freight, labor and energy prices. If inflation in these costs increases beyond our ability to control for them through measures such as implementing operating efficiencies, or we are not able to increase prices to sufficiently offset the effect of various cost increases without negatively impacting customer demand, our margin performance and results of operations would be negatively impacted.
evidence.subsection: Increased prices and inflation could negatively impact our margin performance and our financial results.

evidence.text: Additionally, because not all of our supply arrangements provide for guaranteed supply and some key parts and components may be available only from a single supplier or a limited group of suppliers, we are also subject to supply and pricing risks, which could negatively impact our margin performance.
evidence.subsection: Disruptions in our global supply chain, including product manufacturing and logistical services provided by our supplier partners, may negatively impact our business.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in talent retention, particularly in key areas such as engineering and manufacturing, due to factors like labor shortages and increased turnover, though the extent of these challenges is not fully detailed in the provided text. Additionally, global operations and economic uncertainties may further complicate talent retention efforts by introducing additional risks such as difficulty in staffing and managing operations internationally. Overall, the evidence suggests that talent retention is a significant concern for the company, but the specific details and severity of these challenges remain unclear.

evidence.text: The company is likely facing challenges in talent retention, though specific details are not provided in the text.
evidence.subsection: Our global operations subject us to economic risks.

evidence.text: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business is dependent upon our ability to recruit and retain a workforce with the skills necessary to develop, manufacture and deliver the products and services desired by our customers.
evidence.subsection: Our ability to successfully grow and expand our business depends on our ability to recruit and retain a highly qualified and diverse workforce.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is showing signs of improvement, particularly in non-electronic components, but ongoing challenges with electronic parts and components continue to pose significant disruptions. While efforts to address these issues have been made, the company anticipates that these challenges will persist into 2023, impacting their ability to meet elevated demand.

evidence.text: Supply chain health is improving, but challenges with electronic components persist.

Our ability to meet this elevated level of customer demand improved substantially as the year progressed, due in part to our actions taken to address industry-wide supply-chain challenges (particularly shortages of electronic components), as well as improving availability of non-electronic parts and materials.

While supply chain challenges around the availability of electronic parts and components persist, and will likely continue to impact our ability to meet the elevated levels of demand for our electronic security products into 2023, we remain focused on providing exceptional service and innovation to our customers.
Ongoing supply chain disruptions and delays, material, electronic component and labor shortages have impacted, and may continue to impact, our business, our customers and our suppliers.
